metadata.greeting: Get ready for a fun-filled weekend in London! I've found some amazing activities for your 3-year-old to enjoy, from free museum adventures to exciting Easter egg hunts. With the weather looking great for outdoor play, there's something for everyone to enjoy!

activities.title: Young V&A
activities.description: The Young V&A is a vibrant and interactive museum designed specifically for children and families. This playful space puts children at the forefront with hands-on activities, workshops, and exhibitions.
activities.whyItWorks: Designed with children in mind with dedicated areas for under 2s, hands-on exhibits, and sensory experiences.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Central Line to Bethnal Green. It's a short walk from the station.
transport.tip: The nearest overground station, Cambridge Heath, isn't accessible so avoid this if you have a pushchair.

activities.title: V&A Family Activity: Micro to Macro!
activities.description: Hands-on family event at the Young V&A. Programme is designed for all ages and encourages play and learning.
activities.whyItWorks: Hands-on activities that inspire the whole family.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Central Line to Bethnal Green. It's a short walk from the station.
transport.tip: Check the V&A website for specific times.

activities.title: Discover Children's Story Centre
activities.description: Two floors of immersive play space known as the Story Worlds as well as the Story Garden playground. The museum hosts Baby Storytelling plus crafts and singalong time for babies and toddlers.
activities.whyItWorks: Immersive play spaces and story gardens are perfect for toddlers.

transport.tip: Check the website for event times and booking.

activities.title: Biodiversity Playground
activities.description: A small and beautifully designed wooden playground with a sweet pond theme.
activities.whyItWorks: Perfect for toddlers with its pond theme and wooden structures.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Stratford. It's a short walk from the station.
transport.tip: Combine with a visit to the Olympic Park.

activities.title: Hackney City Farm
activities.description: A small city farm with animals, gardens, and a cafe.
activities.whyItWorks: Animals are always a hit, and it's close to home.

transport.tip: Check for special events on their website.

activities.title: Vauxhall City Farm for Easter Crafts and Animals
activities.price: Free Easter Hunts and Bonnet-Making Crafts
activities.description: Free Easter hunts and bonnet-making crafts. A host of goats, sheep and smaller animals right on your doorstep.
activities.whyItWorks: Great for families and animal-lovers, with a host of goats, sheep and smaller animals.

transport.tip: Located in Zone 1.

activities.title: Epping Forest
activities.description: A large forest with walking trails, playgrounds, and nature.
activities.whyItWorks: Great for outdoor exploration and letting off steam.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Central Line to Epping.
transport.tip: Check the Epping Forest website for specific playground locations.

activities.title: The Very Hungry Caterpillar Trail
activities.description: Children aged 8 and under are invited to discover a variety of illustrated animals nestled amongst the blooming habitats of the reserve, identify the letter on each one and complete the secret word in the activity booklet.
activities.whyItWorks: Discover a variety of illustrated animals nestled amongst the blooming habitats of the reserve.

transport.tip: Check website for details

activities.title: Natural History Museum
activities.dates: Open Daily, Easter Family Festival: 5-20 April 2026
activities.description: Explore dinosaurs, animals, and the natural world.
activities.whyItWorks: Lots to see and do, with hands-on exhibits and an Easter Family Festival.

transport.fromHackney: Take the tube to South Kensington.
transport.tip: It can get very busy, so go early or during off-peak times.

activities.title: Science Museum
activities.description: Discover how the world works with interactive exhibits.
activities.whyItWorks: The Garden and Pattern Pod are great hands-on areas for toddlers.

transport.fromHackney: Take the tube to South Kensington.
transport.tip: Check the website for specific events and activities.

activities.title: Southbank Centre
activities.dates: Various, Spring Family Fun: 1 April - 31 May 2026
activities.description: A riverside hub of arts, fun and food with kids' concerts, festivals, creative workshops, hands-on installations and space to scoot or picnic.
activities.whyItWorks: Lots of free events and activities for families, plus open spaces to roam.

transport.fromHackney: Take the tube to Waterloo or Embankment.
transport.tip: Check the Southbank Centre website for specific event listings.

activities.title: REPLAY: A Limitless Recycled Playground
activities.description: Explore a world of play and invention in a recycled playground for children and their grown-ups.
activities.whyItWorks: Recycled playground perfect for children.

transport.tip: Check website for times.

activities.title: Coram's Fields
activities.description: Seven acres dedicated entirely to children with an adventure playground, sand pits, a paddling pool (summer months), and a small city farm.
activities.whyItWorks: Lots of space to run around and play.

transport.fromHackney: Take the tube to Russell Square or Holborn.
transport.tip: Adults are only allowed entry if accompanying a child.

activities.title: Tower Bridge Easter Family Activities
activities.description: Designed for curious minds and energetic explorers, Tower Bridge's Easter Family Activities are free with admission and combine making, movement and mechanical marvels, giving children the chance to discover how Victorian engineers and inventors shaped the modern world, all while exploring the Bridge from top to bottom.
activities.whyItWorks: Activities combine making, movement and mechanical marvels.

transport.fromHackney: Take the tube to Tower Hill.
transport.tip: Drop-in throughout the day. Included in your visit to Tower Bridge.

activities.title: Cutty Sark Easter Holiday Fun
activities.price: Included with entry to the ship
activities.description: Enjoy workshops, character actors, an Easter egg trail and more at Cutty Sark during the Easter holidays. Suitable for all ages and abilities.
activities.whyItWorks: Character actors and egg trails are great for kids.

transport.tip: Check website for details and schedule.

activities.title: Zoonormous Egg Hunt at London Zoo
activities.description: Search high and low for six giant Easter eggs hidden around the grounds. Each egg reveals a secret letter, encouraging curious minds to work together to uncover the code hidden throughout the Zoo.
activities.whyItWorks: Animals and egg hunt are a winning combination.

transport.fromHackney: Take the tube to Regent's Park or Camden Town.
transport.tip: Book in advance to save money.

activities.title: Easter Quest at Eltham Palace and Gardens
activities.price: Paid, in addition to paid admission
activities.description: Embark on a fun-filled adventure quest around the house and gardens. Follow the Easter trail, hunt for eggs and answer questions along the way to earn a delicious chocolate reward.
activities.whyItWorks: Exploration and chocolate rewards.

transport.tip: Book in advance online.

activities.title: The Lion King
activities.description: Disney's award-winning musical.
activities.whyItWorks: A family-friendly favorite.

transport.fromHackney: Tube to Covent Garden or Charing Cross.
transport.tip: Book in advance for best seats.

activities.title: Hampton Court Palace Easter Events
activities.description: Visit Hampton Court Palace for Easter-themed egg hunts and seasonal workshops. Explore historic rooms, gardens, and the palace grounds while participating in fun activities for children.
activities.whyItWorks: A combination of royal history, spring flowers, and family-friendly events.

transport.tip: Book tickets online in advance.

activities.title: Hobbledown Heath Easter Eggsplorers
activities.description: Easter Eggsplorers returns to Hobbledown Heath from 28th March – 12th April 2026.
activities.whyItWorks: Hunt the Giant Easter Egg Trail, Meet the Easter Bunny, Indoor Playbarn, Easter Entertainment with the Hobblers.

transport.tip: Book online to save money.

activities.title: Croydon's Turf Projects Family Art Fun Day: Build Your Own Cocoon
activities.description: Turf Projects will play host to Family Art Fun Day: Build Your Own Cocoon.
activities.whyItWorks: Great fun with the family.

transport.tip: Book online to save money.

activities.title: Chelsea Physic Garden Easter Egg Trail: the Spring Sprites
activities.price: Paid, in addition to paid admission
activities.description: Follow the trail, search for the eggs and discover the places where the Sprites live. Along the way, complete small tasks and playful challenges to make a special potion to help awaken the Sprites.
activities.whyItWorks: Fun activities, game stations and an exciting egg trail through this glorious garden.

transport.tip: Book online to save money.

activities.title: Lindt Gold Bunny Hunt at Hampton Court Palace
activities.description: Roam around the gardens and find hidden Lindt Gold Bunny statues to win a chocolate treat.
activities.whyItWorks: A reward of chocolate.

transport.tip: Book tickets online in advance.
